title: Verify restructuring works
description: Run import checks, syntax checks, make config to verify pipeline/analysis split

title: Docs + cleanup review + separation-of-concerns audit
description:
After data_loader + paper-script fixes land, do a holistic sweep:

1. Documentation walkthrough — every markdown file must be accurate against the current code layout and data flow. Files to reread with critical eye:
   - README.md, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, STATUS.md, REPLICATION_STATUS.md
   - notes/README.md, notes/CODE_ORGANIZATION.md, notes/table_replication.md
   - analysis/imagequality_classification/README.md
   - analysis/image_survey_classification/CHANGES.md (is it still relevant?)
   - scripts/SPLIT_DATA_README.md (will be deleted with oou, confirm)

2. File cleanup — identify and delete/move unnecessary files:
   - Stale logs/outputs/CSVs in classifier dirs (aggregated_misclassified_*.csv)
   - Old notebooks that no longer run
   - Orphaned __init__.py in empty trees
   - Any scripts referencing deleted paths

3. Separation-of-concerns audit — walk the code and flag any remaining violations:
   - pipeline/ should not consume all_data.json (it produces it)
   - analysis/ should not write to raw/ or intermediate/ (only analysis_output/ and models/)
   - No script should read from or write to both raw/ and intermediate/ and models/ in one pass
   - No second materialization layers between all_data.json and consumers (rule #3)
   - No hard-coded paths outside the Makefile variables
   - Every script that reads data should go through pipeline/data_loader.OrganoidDataset

Produce a punch list of violations found and fix each.
close_reason: Completed across separation-of-concerns work this branch: commits a613a4e (initial cleanup), a45ea5d (deletes/renames + B1/B3/B4), b668358/3688cfa/1e3495c/7f79d72 (C1/C2/C3+C4/C5 splits + dedup), f51794e (final cleanup of orphaned dirs).

title: Investigate R2 mask-data: identify uchicago-masks, masks-renamed, idor-masks origins
description: R2 mask-data has 21,905 files across 4 subdirs vs 3,184 local. Determine what each R2 mask set is, where it came from, and whether any are needed.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: nickross
comments.text:
R2 mask-data has 4 distinct datasets (21,905 total files):

1. idor-masks (2,148 files) = Local masks/manual source data
   - masks-batch-1: 1,035 (manual + threshold) — matches local exactly (365 manual + 670 threshold)
   - masks-batch-2_1: 372 (manual only) — matches local exactly
   - masks-batch-2_2: 741 (manual only) — matches local exactly
   - Missing from R2: local 'Manuais' dir (366 files) and 'Treshold' dir (670 files)

2. masks-renamed (5,154 files) = Predicted masks with normalized names
   - Organized BA1-4/Dy03-30, filenames like BA1_96_1_Dy03_A10_nosplit_nostitch_predmask.png
   - These are small PNG predicted masks (~1.5KB each)
   - Appears to be an older run of predicted masks (not matching current local predicted/)

3. uchicago-masks (10,391 files) = Overlays + predicted masks from UChicago pipeline
   - Organized batch1-4/day03-30/{image_mask_overlays,predicted_masks}
   - image_mask_overlays: overlay PNGs (~300KB each)
   - predicted_masks: predicted mask PNGs (~1.5KB each)
   - These are a DIFFERENT processing run from the current local pipeline

4. uchicago-masks-with-ratio (4,212 files) = Aspect-ratio masks
   - Flat directory, small PNG files (~800 bytes)
   - Filenames: BA1_96_1_Dy03_A2_nosplit_nostitch.png
   - Another processing variant

CONCLUSION: Only idor-masks corresponds to irreplaceable source data (hand-labeled). The other 3 are derived outputs from older pipeline runs. Local masks/manual has 2 extra dirs (Manuais + Treshold) not on R2.

title: Epic: rebuild paper_2026_04 against the data loader
description: Rename current analysis/paper_2026_04 → legacy_paper_2026_04 (frozen reference). Build a new paper_2026_04 from scratch that: (a) uses OrganoidDataset's public API (iter_organoids/organoid_label/include_growth=True) — no _organoids access, no reimplemented growth features. (b) uses centralized LABEL_TO_INT (fixes the inversion bug in legacy perday_image_study where 0=Acceptable contradicts every other script). (c) factors a shared metrics/plotting helper module. (d) reproduces the same outputs (Tables 1+3, Figs 5/8/10/11, perday results.json) so three_model_plot continues to work.

title: Map R2 bucket contents to local DATA_DIR directories

close_reason: Closed

comments.author: nickross
comments.text: Complete map written to DATA_MAP.md. Key findings: R2 uses 6 separate buckets vs local single tree; R2 missing 6 stitched images, 1036 masks, all derived outputs, and has stale all_data.json. R2 has ~20K extra files from older pipeline runs (uchicago-masks, masks-renamed).

title: New metabolites_train.py: use include_growth=True + dedup train_*
description: Two big wins over legacy: (1) Drop _get_growth_features and get_features_with_growth (130 lines). Just call ds.get_metabolite_features(split, day, include_growth=True, include_initial=True). (2) Factor train_lgbm_day + train_logreg_day (180 lines combined) into single train_with_grid_and_threshold(model_factory, param_grid, threshold_grid, scoring, scaler=None) → metrics. Both call sites become ~20 lines.
acceptance_criteria: results.json for Dy24/Dy28/Dy30 within ±0.01 balanced_accuracy of legacy values.

Extend cli.create_summary to accept optional metadata kwargs and merge them into the summary dict; delete dinov2's write_summary.

title: Audit analysis/paper_2026_04/*.py for schema-staleness

STATUS.md claims paper replication (Table 1 metabolite means, Table 3 LightGBM Dy30 bal_acc=0.9444) hit exact match, but that must have been against an older all_data.json in the flat intermediate schema — the current normalized schema breaks every filter in data_loader, and several paper scripts also reach into records directly with rec.get('metabolites') etc.

Audit (after the data_loader fix): every script under analysis/paper_2026_04/ must run end-to-end against today's data/all_data.json and produce numbers matching the STATUS.md claims.

Files to check:
- descriptive_stats.py (Table 1)
- metabolite_boxplot.py (Fig 5)
- metabolites_train.py (Table 3) — has direct rec.get('metabolites') access at line ~119
- feature_importance.py (Fig 10)
- perday_image_study.py (Fig 7 — image model)
- three_model_plot.py (Fig 11)
- generate_splits.py

For any direct record-field access (bypassing OrganoidDataset accessors), switch to the new schema or route through the dataset API.
close_reason: Completed by 1e3495c (C3+C4): all paper_2026_04 scripts rewritten against pipeline.data_loader public API. metabolites_train uses include_growth=True; descriptive_stats uses iter_organoids; perday_image_study uses LABEL_TO_INT (fixes inversion bug). Old scripts frozen in legacy_paper_2026_04/.

Move the dinov2 version into data.py as a public helper that handles both flows: (a) views dict with hard-split fractions (accuracy), (b) OrganoidDataset with fixed splits (dinov2). Should accept either an OrganoidDataset OR a views dict.

close_reason: Closed

comments.author: nickross
comments.text:
R2 has 4 xlsx files, local has 1. Mapping:
- R2 '072325 Metabolite Assays_All Plates with 1103 rerun.xlsx' (3,080,943 bytes) ≈ local 'metabolite_data_07_23_25.xlsx' (3,077,845 bytes) — same date, ~3KB size diff, likely same data with minor edits
- R2 '052225 Metabolite Data_batches 11 and 21.xlsx' — older/subset data (May 2025)
- R2 'Updated Metabolite Data Batches 11_21_22.xlsx' — updated subset
- R2 '384WellAnalysis Metabolite Plate Map and Sample coding.xlsx' — plate map reference doc
R2 extras appear to be earlier versions and reference docs. Local only has the final consolidated file plus derived metabolite_map.json.

(b) plot_balanced_accuracy_by_day(series_dict, output_path) for any subset of {image, lgbm, logreg, combined} models.

title: Paper Reproducibility: Generate all figures, tables, and numbers from all_data.json

All analysis must be self-contained and source from all_data.json. Code from the student branch (2026-winter-promega-final-submission) can be adapted but not copied wholesale. Temporary data from long-running processes may be saved outside the repo.

NOTE: The split files currently live in data/data_splits/. Verify these match the methodology described in the paper or regenerate.

title: Table: Legacy 3-backbone comparison (ViT, ResNet, EfficientNet)
description: Reproduce Table (tab:legacy_model_comparison): cross-model comparison of ViT, ResNet50, EfficientNet-B0. Metrics: Avg TNR (all), Early TNR (Dy3-10), Balanced Accuracy, Days with TNR=0, F1 (Not Acceptable). Paper values: EfficientNet Avg TNR=29.1%, Bal.Acc=59.0%. Source: all_data.json for images+labels, shared splits. This requires TRAINING 3 models (GPU, long-running). Student branch ref: train_model_accuracy.py, generate_efficientnet_summary.py. Output: CSV/table + trained model checkpoints (saved outside repo).
notes: Per-day EfficientNet run on GPU. Code matches student exactly (seed=1, val_acc selection, LR*0.1). Dy28=0.89 bal_acc. Early days show majority-class bias. No ViT/ResNet50 yet.

title: Figure: Per-day vs time-series balanced accuracy comparison
description: Reproduce Figure (perday_vs_timeseries.png): balanced accuracy across culture days at threshold=0.5 for per-day classifier vs time-series classifier. Paper values: per-day peaks at 0.76 (Day 15), 0.80 (Day 20.5), 0.87 (Day 30); time-series stays near/below 0.50 until late days. Source: all_data.json for images+labels. Requires training per-day EfficientNet models AND time-series (effnet_ts) models for each day. Student branch ref: run_per_day_study.py. GPU required, long-running.
notes: Per-day results done. Time-series model (CNN-LSTM or temporal attention) not yet implemented. Per-day figure generated.

title: Table: Metabolite LogReg vs LightGBM comparison
description: Reproduce Table (tab:metabolite_model_comparison): LR vs LightGBM aggregated across 11 days. Paper values: LightGBM Avg Bal.Acc=60.9%, Avg Recall(NA)=39.3%, Best Bal.Acc=94.4% (Day 30); LogReg Avg Bal.Acc=52.9%. Source: all_data.json metabolite records + labels + shared splits. Requires training 22 models (11 days x 2 algorithms). Student branch ref: train_metabolites_cpu.py, train_metabolites_logreg.py. CPU-only, moderate runtime.
close_reason: Done: LightGBM Dy30=0.9444 bal_acc matches paper. LR uses f1_weighted/saga/l1+l2 matching student code

title: Figure: LightGBM vs Logistic Regression balanced accuracy by day
description: Reproduce Figure (LightGBM_vs_Logistic_Regression.png): balanced accuracy by day for both metabolite models with shaded late-stage region. Source: results from metabolite model training. Can be generated from the same run as the metabolite comparison table.

title: Figure: LightGBM feature importance across days

Key findings: glutamate/lactate important early, malate difference most important at Day 30. Source: trained LightGBM models from metabolite training. Student branch ref: extract_feature_importance.py.

title: Figure: Three-model comparison (image, metabolite, combined)
description: Reproduce Figure (three_model.png): balanced accuracy comparison across days for image-only, metabolite-only, and combined models. Paper: image peaks at Day 15 (~0.76) and Day 30 (~0.88); metabolite strongest at Day 30 (~0.94); combined tracks metabolite early, ~0.76 late. Source: results from all three model families. This is the capstone figure requiring all model training to be complete.

title: Combined model: PCA image features + metabolite LightGBM
description: Train per-day combined LightGBM classifiers: 12 PCA-reduced EfficientNet embeddings + 10 metabolite features = 22-dim vector. GridSearchCV with StratifiedGroupKFold (3 folds), F1(NA) metric. Source: all_data.json for images+metabolites+labels. Requires pre-trained EfficientNet for embeddings. Student branch ref: train_combined_lgbm.py. GPU for embeddings, CPU for LightGBM.

title: Delete data_splits/ materialization (AGENTS.md rule #3 violation)
description:
AGENTS.md rule #3: all_data.json is the single source of truth. Do not materialize filtered/transformed data into separate JSON files for downstream models; use the data loader instead.

scripts/split_data_reproducible.py and scripts/split_data_no_stitch.py violate this. They write data_splits/{both,image}_{train,val,test}_{base,switch1,switch2,switch3}.json containing fully-materialized per-organoid views with absolute /net/projects2/promega paths baked in. Files break on any clone and drift from the true source.

Only consumer: analysis/imagequality_classification/train_model_accuracy_tony_dinov2.py (reads both_{train,val,test}_base.json).

Steps (after data_loader schema fix lands):
1. Delete data/data_splits/*.json (root data_splits/ already removed)
2. Add data_splits/ and data/data_splits/ to .gitignore
3. Rewrite train_model_accuracy_tony_dinov2.py to use OrganoidDataset + filters_for_mode('base') from pipeline/data_loader.py
4. Update analysis/imagequality_classification/run_tony_dinov2_fixed_splits.s to drop split-file args
5. Delete scripts/split_data_reproducible.py, scripts/split_data_no_stitch.py, scripts/SPLIT_DATA_README.md
6. Grep for any remaining references to data_splits/ and clean up

Note: filters_for_mode() covering base/switch1/switch2/switch3 is already added to pipeline/data_loader.py in this branch.
close_reason: data_splits/ already gone (.gitignored, dirs absent on disk). scripts/split_data_*.py and SPLIT_DATA_README.md deleted. Sole consumer train_metabolites.py frozen as metabolites_legacy/. data/data_splits/ also absent.

title: Verify: paper outputs match legacy within tolerance

acceptance_criteria: All 5 outputs match legacy within stated tolerance.

acceptance_criteria: Smoke-test: run produces metabolite_summary_table.csv with same values as legacy version (within rounding).

title: Verify Makefile data paths are consistent end-to-end

title: Epic: split multimodal/train_multimodal.py (1030 → orchestrator)
description: Apply imagequality_classification template + dedup. Target: <100-line orchestrator, ~6 sibling modules. Three real wins beyond the split: (1) collapse 6-branch batch-unpacking conditionals in train_epoch/eval_epoch — they all do '*inputs, y = batch'. (2) Merge eval_epoch_detailed into eval_epoch (110-line near-duplicate). (3) Move metabolite-padding out of MetaboliteBranch.forward (it belongs in the dataset, not the model).

title: Audit and annotate experimental classifier scripts
description: Classifier dir has train_model_enhanced.py, train_model_soft_labels.py, train_model_deep_ensemble.py, train_model_accuracy_tony_dinov2.py plus many output dirs. Document which are main vs experimental in classifier README.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix data_loader.py: align with normalized all_data.json schema
description:
pipeline/data_loader.py was written against the flat in-memory intermediate dict inside merge_all_data.py (fields BA, dayID, processed.*, metabolites plural). The actual on-disk all_data.json is the nested normalized schema produced by normalized_records.OrganoidRecordBuilder (plate.batch, day.id, images.*, metabolite singular). Against today's all_data.json the data_loader returns zero organoids — every filter rejects every record.

Field translations needed:
- rec['BA'] -> rec['plate']['batch'] (note: batch is 'BA1 96_1' form, not just 'BA1')
- rec['dayID'] -> rec['day']['id']
- rec['processed']['img_path'] -> rec['images']['img_path']
- rec['processed']['mask_path'] -> rec['images']['mask_path']
- rec['processed']['overlay_path'] -> rec['images']['overlay_path']
- rec['metabolites'] -> rec['metabolite']
- survey/label shape needs verification against OrganoidRecordBuilder._build_surveys output
- _extract_organoid_id() regex on key is now redundant — use rec['organoid_id'] directly

Acceptance: analysis/paper_2026_04/descriptive_stats.py runs end-to-end and reproduces Table 1 (all 5 metabolite means match paper). Sanity-check Table 3 LightGBM Dy30 = 0.9444.
close_reason: Resolved earlier in branch (commits 8cdaff4, af20cd1, e12dbf9) — data_loader reads normalized schema correctly. Verified via descriptive_stats end-to-end run this session: Glucose mean 11.622, all 5 metabolite means within 0.01 of paper values.

title: Thread loss_fn + scheduler_factory through train.run_phases
description: train_model_dinov2.py defines its own run_phases (lines 95-127, 33 lines) just to inject FocalLoss + ReduceLROnPlateau. The shared train.run_phases (analysis/imagequality_classification/train.py) already accepts loss_fn=None via epoch_loop. Extend it to also accept an optional scheduler_factory(opt) callable; default behavior unchanged. Then delete the dinov2-local run_phases.
acceptance_criteria: train_model_dinov2.py imports run_phases from .train; local run_phases removed; both train_model_accuracy and train_model_dinov2 produce identical metrics on the same input.

title: New perday_image_study.py: fix label inversion + use shared metrics
description: Legacy line 227: '0 if lbl == "Acceptable" else 1' inverts every other script's convention. Use LABEL_TO_INT consistently (1=Acceptable). Use common.compute_classification_metrics (drops local compute_metrics).
acceptance_criteria: perday_results.json matches legacy after accounting for label flip; downstream three_model_plot.py still produces correct plot.
